message.content:
Implement EXACTLY ONE task end-to-end in your isolated git worktree, run the gate, commit on the task branch, return the structured result JSON. Do NOT mutate the ledger; do NOT merge.

Task id: T79 (ledgerRefs goals:G5)
Branch: implement/T79    Base commit: a146fde (current main HEAD). If cut from a stale HEAD, `git reset --hard a146fde` first; confirm docs/design.md, README.md, wanbond.example.toml, docs/install.md, and internal/config (the [dns] block + per-peer dns opt-in from G5) exist before starting.
headline: Sync docs and example config: DNS endpoints, resolver privacy trade-offs
description: Per AGENTS.md (docs in sync with code) close G5 with a doc sweep: README.md and docs/design.md gain the DNS-endpoints section — opt-in posture and WHY default-off (the DPI thesis: a pre-tunnel cleartext signal naming a blocklistable host), the exact leaked artifact per resolver mode (system: plaintext query; DoH/DoT: SNI + timing to the provider — cite the tested statements from the T76 DPI-posture task, which documented these in test/e2e/p5_dpi_test.go), defer-and-reconcile boot semantics, re-resolution cadence + liveness-loss trigger + change suppression, multi-record expansion feeding hub-failover, and the mixing rules with ordered endpoints. wanbond.example.toml gains a commented hostname-endpoint peer plus a full [dns] block (system, doh, dot variants, incl. the bootstrap_ip requirement). docs/install.md if it documents config fields. Verify the example still loads.
acceptance: The example config parses: the existing example-config test (or a one-line addition) round-trips wanbond.example.toml through config.Load with the new block commented-in variant covered; README/design.md sections exist and name the per-mode leaked artifacts; go test ./... passes.

Gate (run via `nix develop -c sh -c '<cmd>'` — go not on bare PATH): `go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...`. Read internal/config for the exact [dns] field names (system/doh/dot, bootstrap_ip, poll interval/timeout duration-strings) and the per-peer dns=true + hostname endpoint syntax so the example is ACCURATE and LOADABLE. Find the existing example-config round-trip test (likely internal/config) and ensure wanbond.example.toml still config.Load()s (add the commented-in [dns] variant coverage if the test supports it). Match the existing README/design.md section style.
If your worktree is not reachable for edits, `git switch -c t79-fix` off a146fde in your own worktree via the shared .git store, commit, report the EXACT sha. Return {taskId, status, resultCommit, branch, filesTouched, checkSummary, summary, blockedReason?} with the exact resultCommit sha.

- cq:advance: Advance the WHOLE flow one cycle — chain /cq:investigate:advance, /cq:plan:advance, /cq:implement:advance to quiescence, then report DRAINED / BLOCKED-ON-QUESTIONS / MIXED.
- cq:implement-review

- cq:plan: Start a plan-flow goal — create the goal (from free text OR one-or-more idea-ids, one goal per idea), then hand off to the planner for the first clarifying questions.
- cq:plan-review
- cq:plan:advance: Advance plan-flow goals one full round — a given goal, or (no argument) every unlocked goal — running the planner↔reviewer loop until each needs the user or reaches `planned`.
- cq:plan:follow-up
- cq:planners
- cq:reviewers
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- codex:rescue
- codex:setup
- codex:codex-cli-runtime

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

```go
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64
78		// RaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/above which M may rise. In
79		// (LowerThreshold, 1).
80		RaiseThreshold float64
81		// LowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/below which M may fall. In
82		// [0, RaiseThreshold). The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis deadband.
83		LowerThreshold float64
84		// SafetyFactor is the headroom multiplier on the smoothed loss the parity is
85		// sized to mask. >= 1 in the SafetyFactor sizing mode (TargetResidual unset);
86		// MUST be 0 when TargetResidual is set (the two modes are mutually exclusive).
87		SafetyFactor float64
88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.
95		TargetResidual float64
96		// MaxStep bounds |ΔM| applied per RateInterval. >= 1.
97		MaxStep int
98		// RateInterval is the minimum time between two M changes. > 0.
99		RateInterval time.Duration
100		// Dwell is the minimum time smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold
101		// before the first decrease. >= 0.
102		Dwell time.Duration
103	}
104	
105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
171	
```

```go
66	// construction (fail-fast): a mis-tuned band or an out-of-field parity bound is
67	// rejected by NewController rather than producing undefined control behavior at
68	// runtime.
69	type Config struct {
70		// DataShards is K, the fixed group size the controller sizes parity for. >= 1.
71		DataShards int
72		// MaxParity is the upper bound on the emitted M. >= 1 and DataShards+MaxParity
73		// <= MaxShards (the Reed-Solomon field limit).
74		MaxParity int
75		// Alpha is the EWMA weight of the newest loss sample, in (0,1]. Smaller = more
76		// smoothing.
77		Alpha float64
78		// RaiseThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/above which M may rise. In
79		// (LowerThreshold, 1).
80		RaiseThreshold float64
81		// LowerThreshold is the smoothed-loss level at/below which M may fall. In
82		// [0, RaiseThreshold). The gap to RaiseThreshold is the hysteresis deadband.
83		LowerThreshold float64
84		// SafetyFactor is the headroom multiplier on the smoothed loss the parity is
85		// sized to mask. >= 1 in the SafetyFactor sizing mode (TargetResidual unset);
86		// MUST be 0 when TargetResidual is set (the two modes are mutually exclusive).
87		SafetyFactor float64
88		// TargetResidual, when set (> 0), selects the RESIDUAL-SLA sizing mode and is
89		// the PRIMARY parity surface: instead of the bare SafetyFactor multiplier the
90		// controller derives M by inverting the binomial residual model
91		// E[max(0,D-M)]/K (D ~ Bin(K, smoothed loss), see residual.go) to the smallest
92		// M whose modeled residual is <= TargetResidual, clamped to [0, MaxParity]. It
93		// SUPERSEDES SafetyFactor (which must be 0 when this is set). Must be in (0,1)
94		// when set; 0 selects the legacy SafetyFactor path.
95		TargetResidual float64
96		// MaxStep bounds |ΔM| applied per RateInterval. >= 1.
97		MaxStep int
98		// RateInterval is the minimum time between two M changes. > 0.
99		RateInterval time.Duration
100		// Dwell is the minimum time smoothed loss must stay at/below LowerThreshold
101		// before the first decrease. >= 0.
102		Dwell time.Duration
103	}
104	
105	// DefaultConfig returns a Config populated from the documented default control
106	// constants.
107	func DefaultConfig() Config {
108		return Config{
109			DataShards:     DefaultDataShards,
110			MaxParity:      DefaultMaxParity,
111			Alpha:          DefaultAlpha,
112			RaiseThreshold: DefaultRaiseThreshold,
113			LowerThreshold: DefaultLowerThreshold,
114			SafetyFactor:   DefaultSafetyFactor,
115			MaxStep:        DefaultMaxStep,
116			RateInterval:   DefaultRateInterval,
117			Dwell:          DefaultDwell,
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Validate enforces the control-law invariants, failing fast so a mis-tuned
122	// controller is rejected at construction rather than misbehaving at runtime.
123	func (c Config) Validate() error {
124		if c.DataShards < 1 {
125			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards (K) must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
126		}
127		if c.MaxParity < 1 {
128			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxParity must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxParity)
129		}
130		if c.DataShards+c.MaxParity > MaxShards {
131			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: DataShards+MaxParity must be <= %d (Reed-Solomon field limit), got %d", MaxShards, c.DataShards+c.MaxParity)
132		}
133		if !(c.Alpha > 0 && c.Alpha <= 1) || math.IsNaN(c.Alpha) {
134			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Alpha must be in (0,1], got %g", c.Alpha)
135		}
136		if math.IsNaN(c.LowerThreshold) || c.LowerThreshold < 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold must be >= 0, got %g", c.LowerThreshold)
138		}
139		if math.IsNaN(c.RaiseThreshold) || c.RaiseThreshold >= 1 {
140			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RaiseThreshold must be < 1, got %g", c.RaiseThreshold)
141		}
142		if c.LowerThreshold >= c.RaiseThreshold {
143			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: LowerThreshold (%g) must be < RaiseThreshold (%g) so the hysteresis deadband is non-empty", c.LowerThreshold, c.RaiseThreshold)
144		}
145		if math.IsNaN(c.TargetResidual) {
146			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be a number, got NaN")
147		}
148		if c.TargetResidual != 0 {
149			// Residual-SLA sizing mode: TargetResidual governs and SafetyFactor is inert,
150			// so the two must not both be set (mutually exclusive, fail-fast).
151			if c.TargetResidual < 0 || c.TargetResidual >= 1 {
152				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: TargetResidual must be in (0,1) when set, got %g", c.TargetResidual)
153			}
154			if c.SafetyFactor != 0 {
155				return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor and TargetResidual are mutually exclusive (TargetResidual is the primary residual-SLA surface); leave SafetyFactor 0 when TargetResidual is set, got SafetyFactor=%g", c.SafetyFactor)
156			}
157		} else if math.IsNaN(c.SafetyFactor) || math.IsInf(c.SafetyFactor, 0) || c.SafetyFactor < 1 {
158			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: SafetyFactor must be a finite value >= 1 (or set TargetResidual instead), got %g", c.SafetyFactor)
159		}
160		if c.MaxStep < 1 {
161			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: MaxStep must be >= 1, got %d", c.MaxStep)
162		}
163		if c.RateInterval <= 0 {
164			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: RateInterval must be > 0, got %s", c.RateInterval)
165		}
166		if c.Dwell < 0 {
167			return fmt.Errorf("adaptivefec: Dwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.Dwell)
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
171	
```

26		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
27			return nil, 0, false, err
28		}

31			return nil, 0, false, fmt.Errorf("dnsresolve: no such host %q", host)
32		}

34	}

12	// across the codebase inject it in place of SystemResolver.
13	type FakeResolver struct {
14		// Hosts maps a hostname to the addrs Lookup returns for it, in order.
15		Hosts map[string][]netip.Addr
16		// MinTTL and TTLOk are returned verbatim for every mapped host.
17		MinTTL time.Duration
18		TTLOk  bool
19	}

22	
23	// Lookup implements Resolver: it returns the addrs mapped to host, or a
24	// non-nil error when host is not in Hosts.
25	func (f *FakeResolver) Lookup(ctx context.Context, host string) ([]netip.Addr, time.Duration, bool, error) {
26		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
27			return nil, 0, false, err
28		}
29		addrs, ok := f.Hosts[host]
30		if !ok {
31			return nil, 0, false, fmt.Errorf("dnsresolve: no such host %q", host)
32		}
33		return addrs, f.MinTTL, f.TTLOk, nil
34	}

22	
23	// Requirement-6 (DPI-resistance) EXTERNAL-TOOL non-classification check (M9).
24	//
25	// TestWireFormatAudit (T26) proves the obfuscation properties of the wanbond wire
26	// PROGRAMMATICALLY (entropy + no fixed-offset fingerprint). This test complements it
27	// by running the CAPTURED wire through the two production DPI engines an adversary on
28	// a hostile network would use — nDPI (ndpiReader) and Suricata — and asserting NEITHER
29	// classifies the obfuscated wanbond flow as WireGuard or any identified VPN by PAYLOAD.
30	//
31	// PAYLOAD vs PORT — the decisive distinction. Requirement 6 is about the WIRE FORMAT /
32	// PAYLOAD being indistinguishable from random, NOT about the UDP port (wanbond's listen
33	// port is operator-configurable). nDPI (and DPI engines generally) will PORT-GUESS
34	// "WireGuard"/VPN for ANY UDP flow on WireGuard's IANA-registered port 51820 regardless
35	// of payload — a benign [Confidence: Match by port] classification that says nothing
36	// about the wire. (Reproduced with nDPI 5.0: a random-payload UDP flow to :51820 is
37	// labelled WireGuard/VPN "Match by port"; the SAME payload to :40000 is "Unknown".) So
38	// this test isolates PAYLOAD DPI-resistance two ways, BOTH applied:
39	//
40	//	(a) it parses nDPI's PER-FLOW output and reads the [Confidence: …] field, failing
41	//	    ONLY on a WireGuard/VPN classification whose confidence is PAYLOAD/CONTENT-based
42	//	    (e.g. "DPI") — a "Match by port" guess is EXPECTED and never fails; and
43	//	(b) it captures the wanbond flow on a NON-registered UDP port (dpiListenPort), so
44	//	    nDPI's port guess does not fire at all and any WireGuard/VPN label on that
45	//	    capture is unambiguously a genuine payload-fingerprint leak.
46	//
47	// Symmetry: the positive control (plain WireGuard) is detected by exactly the
48	// payload/content confidence class ("DPI") that the negative leg must be FREE of, so the
49	// two legs are the same predicate — one asserted present, one asserted absent.
50	//
51	// NON-VACUITY / POSITIVE CONTROL. A bare "not detected" result is meaningless unless the
52	// detector demonstrably DETECTS WireGuard BY PAYLOAD when it is present. wanbond ALWAYS
53	// obfuscates via its outer frame codec, so a plain-WG reference cannot come from wanbond
54	// itself. Option (a) of the task — plain amneziawg-go in-process via tun/netstack — is
55	// NOT tractable: netstack pulls gVisor, whose vendored tcpip/stack fails to build under
56	// the go toolchain (bridge_test.go declares `package bridge_test`), and two kernel-TUN
57	// WireGuard endpoints in ONE netns short-circuit (a packet to the peer's on-link /32
58	// never traverses the WG socket). So this uses option (b): a COMMITTED genuine
59	// plain-WireGuard capture (testdata/plain-wireguard.pcap, real kernel-WG handshake +
60	// transport). nDPI classifies it WireGuard with [Confidence: DPI]; the positive control
61	// asserts that PAYLOAD-confidence classification FIRST — if it is absent, the tool or
62	// parser is broken and the negative assertion is vacuous.
63	//
64	// nDPI carries the WG-SPECIFIC positive control. Suricata 8.x's default configuration
65	// ships NO WireGuard app-layer parser or signature (it labels even a plain-WG flow
66	// app_proto="failed"), so it cannot serve as a WG positive control; its value here is
67	// the app-layer/anomaly NEGATIVE check (with a ">=1 flow decoded" sanity so a zero-decode
68	// run cannot pass silently). This is DOCUMENTED (docs/install.md Limitations and the log
69	// lines below); the suricata positive run is informational only.
70	//
71	// PRIVILEGE. The wanbond capture reuses T26's tcpdump plumbing (startPcap; note the
72	// deliberate absence of `-Z root`, which segfaults tcpdump 4.99.x) over the netns/netem
73	// fixture, so this test needs root/CAP_NET_ADMIN and runs only on the e2e host. It also
74	// needs ndpiReader and suricata ON PATH; if either is absent it FAILS LOUD (never
75	// t.Skip — a skipped DPI check is false assurance). The e2e host must have nDPI and
76	// Suricata provisioned (the orchestrator does this).
77	const (
78		// ndpiReaderBin / suricataBin are the external DPI engines, resolved on PATH.
79		ndpiReaderBin = "ndpiReader"
80		suricataBin   = "suricata"
81	
82		// dpiToolTimeout bounds each external-tool invocation so a hung ndpiReader/suricata
83		// fails the test rather than wedging the suite.
84		dpiToolTimeout = 90 * time.Second
85	
86		// dpiLoadSecs is the bulk-transfer duration driving the wanbond capture: long enough
87		// to put DATA/PARITY/PROBE frames + amnezia junk on the wire for the engines to
88		// inspect, short enough to keep the test quick.
89		dpiLoadSecs = 5
90	
91		// dpiListenPort is the wanbond WireGuard listen port used for the P5 capture. It is a
92		// NON-registered high port ON PURPOSE: capturing on WireGuard's IANA-registered 51820
93		// (the shared listenPort) would make nDPI PORT-GUESS "WireGuard"/VPN regardless of
94		// payload, conflating a port guess with a genuine payload leak. On a non-registered
95		// port nDPI's port guess never fires, so any WireGuard/VPN classification of this
96		// capture is unambiguously a payload-fingerprint defect. wanbond's port is
97		// operator-configurable, so this changes nothing about the acceptance semantics.
98		dpiListenPort = 40000
99	
100		// plainWGPcap is the committed positive-control capture (real plain WireGuard).
101		plainWGPcap = "testdata/plain-wireguard.pcap"
102	
103		// suricataConfigEnv optionally overrides the suricata.yaml path; defaultSuricataConfig
104		// is the standard location a provisioned suricata install writes. When neither is
105		// present, suricata falls back to its own built-in config search.
106		suricataConfigEnv     = "WANBOND_SURICATA_CONFIG"
107		defaultSuricataConfig = "/etc/suricata/suricata.yaml"
108	
109		// suricataEveFile is the JSON event log suricata writes into its -l directory.
110		suricataEveFile = "eve.json"
111	
112		// dpiDNSGuardFilter is the tcpdump BPF filter for the zero-DNS-egress guard
113		// (Q29/Q33): the well-known ports for cleartext system DNS (53), DNS-over-TLS (853),
114		// and DNS-over-HTTPS (443 — shared with generic HTTPS, but this fixture's edge netns
115		// makes no other outbound connection during the capture window, so any packet on
116		// these ports here IS DNS-adjacent egress, in either direction). This capture runs on
117		// a DNS-OFF config (no peer carries the `dns = true` opt-in; every endpoint is an
118		// IP literal), so Q29 requires it to be PROVABLY inert: zero packets on any of these
119		// ports for the whole session. Documents the exact artifact each mode would otherwise
120		// leak to a passive observer, once DNS IS opted in: system mode leaks a CLEARTEXT DNS
121		// query naming the concentrator's hostname; DoH/DoT mode leaks the TLS ClientHello
122		// SNI plus connection timing to the resolver (the query payload itself is encrypted,
123		// but the SNI and the timing correlate the edge to that resolver).
124		dpiDNSGuardFilter = "port 53 or port 853 or port 443"
125	)
126	
127	// vpnLabelPatterns are the lowercase substrings that mark a DPI label as a WireGuard or
128	// otherwise-identified VPN classification. A PAYLOAD-confidence hit on the OBFUSCATED
129	// wanbond flow is a requirement-6 defect; the same on the plain-WG positive control is
130	// the expected teeth. A classification of Unknown/QUIC/DNS/etc. matches none of these.
131	var vpnLabelPatterns = []string{"wireguard", "openvpn", "ipsec", "tunnel", "vpn"}
132	
133	// matchVPNLabel reports the first vpnLabelPattern contained (case-insensitively) in s.
134	func matchVPNLabel(s string) (pattern string, ok bool) {
135		l := strings.ToLower(s)
136		for _, p := range vpnLabelPatterns {
137			if strings.Contains(l, p) {
138				return p, true
139			}
140		}
141		return "", false
142	}
143	
144	func TestP5DPI(t *testing.T) {
145		requireDPITool(t, ndpiReaderBin)
146		requireDPITool(t, suricataBin)
147	
148		bin := buildWanbond(t)
149		top := SetupWithPaths(t, []pathSpec{auditPath})
150	
151		// (1) POSITIVE CONTROL FIRST — prove the detector has PAYLOAD teeth before trusting a
152		// "not classified" result. nDPI must classify the known plain-WireGuard capture as
153		// WireGuard/VPN with a PAYLOAD/CONTENT confidence (not a mere port guess); if it does
154		// not, the tool or parser is broken and every negative below is vacuous.
155		plainPath := plainWGPcapPath(t)
156		posFlows := runNdpiFlows(t, plainPath)
157		if f, label, pat, ok := payloadVPNFlow(posFlows); !ok {
158			t.Fatalf("POSITIVE CONTROL FAILED: nDPI did not classify the known plain-WireGuard capture %s as WireGuard/VPN by PAYLOAD (flows: %s). "+
159				"The detector or the per-flow parser is broken — the negative assertions would be vacuous.", plainPath, formatFlows(posFlows))
160		} else {
161			t.Logf("positive control OK: nDPI classified plain WireGuard as %q by payload (matched %q, %s)", label, pat, f.String())
162		}
163	
164		// (2) Capture the OBFUSCATED wanbond flow (amnezia junk + FEC parity active) on a
165		// NON-registered port and run both engines over it. Neither may classify it
166		// WireGuard/VPN by payload. The SAME session also runs the zero-DNS-egress guard
167		// (Q29/Q33): this config carries no `dns = true` opt-in (every endpoint is an IP
168		// literal), so NO packet may appear on the system-DNS/DoT/DoH ports for the whole
169		// capture window — proving DNS stays inert on the wire, not just in-process (see
170		// the tripwire-resolver unit test in internal/device for the in-process half).
171		wbPcap, dnsGuardPcap := captureWanbondFlow(t, top, bin)
172	
173		assertZeroDNSEgress(t, dnsGuardPcap)
174	
175		negFlows := runNdpiFlows(t, wbPcap)
176		if f, label, pat, ok := payloadVPNFlow(negFlows); ok {
177			t.Fatalf("REQUIREMENT-6 DEFECT: nDPI classified the OBFUSCATED wanbond flow as a WireGuard/VPN protocol BY PAYLOAD: label %q (matched %q), %s. "+
178				"The flow was captured on non-registered port %d, so this is NOT a port guess — the amnezia+FEC obfuscation is leaking a DPI-identifiable payload fingerprint. "+
179				"Fix the codec; do NOT weaken this matcher. All nDPI flows: %s",
180				label, pat, f.String(), dpiListenPort, formatFlows(negFlows))
181		}
182		t.Logf("nDPI negative OK: obfuscated wanbond flow on port %d — no PAYLOAD WireGuard/VPN classification (flows: %s)", dpiListenPort, formatFlows(negFlows))
183	
184		appProtos, signatures, flows := runSuricata(t, wbPcap)
185		if flows == 0 {
186			t.Fatalf("suricata decoded ZERO flows from the wanbond capture %s — a zero-decode run cannot certify 'no WireGuard/VPN' (check the capture / suricata config), not a pass.", wbPcap)
187		}
188		for _, ap := range appProtos {
189			if pat, ok := matchVPNLabel(ap); ok {
190				t.Fatalf("REQUIREMENT-6 DEFECT: suricata labelled the obfuscated wanbond flow app_proto=%q (matched %q) — a DPI-identifiable VPN fingerprint leaked.", ap, pat)
191			}
192		}
193		for _, sig := range signatures {
194			if pat, ok := matchVPNLabel(sig); ok {
195				t.Fatalf("REQUIREMENT-6 DEFECT: suricata raised a WireGuard/VPN alert on the obfuscated wanbond flow: %q (matched %q).", sig, pat)
196			}
197		}
198		t.Logf("suricata negative OK: %d flow(s) decoded, app_protos=%v, %d alerts — none WireGuard/VPN", flows, appProtos, len(signatures))
199	
200		// (3) Suricata positive is INFORMATIONAL (see the file doc): its default config has
201		// no WG parser, so nDPI carries the WG-specific positive control. Record whichever
202		// outcome this suricata build gives so a provisioned WG ruleset shows teeth.
203		posAppProtos, posSignatures, _ := runSuricata(t, plainPath)
204		if labelsHitVPN(posAppProtos) || labelsHitVPN(posSignatures) {
205			t.Logf("suricata positive (bonus teeth): classified the known plain-WireGuard capture as WireGuard/VPN (app_protos=%v, alerts=%v)", posAppProtos, posSignatures)
206		} else {
207			t.Logf("suricata has NO WireGuard app-layer parser/signature in this build (plain-WG app_protos=%v) — this is expected for the stock suricata config; "+
208				"nDPI carries the WG-specific positive control, suricata provides the app-layer/anomaly negative check (documented in docs/install.md Limitations).", posAppProtos)
209		}
210	}

212	// requireDPITool fails the test LOUD if name is not on PATH. A DPI check that silently
213	// skips when its engine is missing is false assurance, so this never t.Skip()s.
214	func requireDPITool(t *testing.T, name string) {
215		t.Helper()
216		if _, err := exec.LookPath(name); err != nil {
217			t.Fatalf("%s not found on PATH: the P5 DPI non-classification check requires it. "+
218				"Provision nDPI/Suricata on this host (the check is never skipped — a skipped DPI check is false assurance). lookup error: %v", name, err)
219		}
220	}
221	
222	// plainWGPcapPath resolves the committed positive-control capture to an absolute path
223	// and fails if it is missing.
224	func plainWGPcapPath(t *testing.T) string {
225		t.Helper()
226		abs, err := filepath.Abs(plainWGPcap)
227		if err != nil {
228			t.Fatalf("resolve %s: %v", plainWGPcap, err)
229		}
230		if _, err := os.Stat(abs); err != nil {
231			t.Fatalf("positive-control capture %s missing (regenerate with testdata/gen-plain-wireguard.sh): %v", abs, err)
232		}
233		return abs
234	}
235	
236	// captureWanbondFlow brings up one fresh amnezia+FEC wanbond tunnel over auditPath on the
237	// NON-registered dpiListenPort, captures the outer UDP wire on the edge veth with tcpdump
238	// (T26's startPcap — no `-Z root`) while a short bulk transfer drives the full
239	// DATA/PARITY/PROBE/junk mix, and returns the path to the completed pcap savefile PLUS the
240	// path to a second, concurrent capture over the SAME veth/window restricted to the
241	// system-DNS/DoT/DoH ports (dpiDNSGuardFilter) — the zero-DNS-egress guard's evidence. The
242	// tunnel config carries no `dns = true` opt-in, so the second capture must be empty.
243	func captureWanbondFlow(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string) (wbPcap, dnsGuardPcap string) {
244		t.Helper()
245		pcapFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "wanbond-obfuscated.pcap")
246		dnsPcapFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "wanbond-dns-guard.pcap")
247		cap := top.startPcap(t, auditPath.edgeVeth, dpiListenPort, pcapFile)
248		dnsCap := top.startPcapFilter(t, auditPath.edgeVeth, dpiDNSGuardFilter, dnsPcapFile)
249	
250		edge, conc := setupWanbondDPITunnel(t, top, bin, dpiListenPort)
251		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
252			cap.stop(t)
253			dnsCap.stop(t)
254			t.Fatalf("wanbond tunnel never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
255		}
256		if mbps := top.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, dpiLoadSecs); mbps <= 0 {
257			cap.stop(t)
258			dnsCap.stop(t)
259			t.Fatalf("wanbond capture: non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", mbps)
260		}
261		cap.stop(t)
262		dnsCap.stop(t)
263	
264		if fi, err := os.Stat(pcapFile); err != nil || fi.Size() == 0 {
265			t.Fatalf("wanbond capture %s is missing or empty (err=%v)\n--- tcpdump ---\n%s", pcapFile, err, cap.log())
266		}
267		if fi, err := os.Stat(dnsPcapFile); err != nil || fi.Size() == 0 {
268			t.Fatalf("DNS-guard capture %s is missing or empty (err=%v) — the savefile must at least contain a global header even with zero packets\n--- tcpdump ---\n%s", dnsPcapFile, err, dnsCap.log())
269		}
270		return pcapFile, dnsPcapFile
271	}

273	// assertZeroDNSEgress reads the zero-DNS-egress guard's capture (dpiDNSGuardFilter,
274	// system-DNS/DoT/DoH ports) and fails the test if it holds ANY packet: with DNS opted
275	// off, the edge netns must emit and receive NOTHING on those ports (Q29). Once DNS IS
276	// opted in, this is exactly what would leak: system mode, a cleartext DNS query naming
277	// the concentrator's hostname; DoH/DoT mode, the TLS ClientHello SNI + timing to the
278	// resolver.
279	func assertZeroDNSEgress(t *testing.T, dnsGuardPcap string) {
280		t.Helper()
281		data, err := os.ReadFile(dnsGuardPcap)
282		if err != nil {
283			t.Fatalf("read DNS-guard capture %s: %v", dnsGuardPcap, err)
284		}
285		n, err := wireaudit.CountPcapPackets(data)
286		if err != nil {
287			t.Fatalf("parse DNS-guard capture %s: %v", dnsGuardPcap, err)
288		}
289		if n != 0 {
290			t.Fatalf("REQUIREMENT Q29/Q33 DEFECT: %d packet(s) on the system-DNS/DoT/DoH ports (53/853/443) egressed the edge netns "+
291				"on a DNS-off config (no peer opts in `dns = true`, every endpoint is an IP literal). DNS must stay PROVABLY inert on the "+
292				"wire: with DNS opted in, this is exactly what leaks — system mode, a cleartext DNS query naming the concentrator's "+
293				"hostname; DoH/DoT mode, the TLS ClientHello SNI + connection timing to the resolver.", n)
294		}
295		t.Logf("zero-DNS-egress guard OK: 0 packets on ports 53/853/443 over the wanbond capture window (DNS opt-in off)")
296	}

298	// setupWanbondDPITunnel brings the edge+concentrator tunnel up over auditPath with BOTH
299	// the amnezia obfuscation profile (junk active) AND the fixed-ratio FEC plane (parity

303	func setupWanbondDPITunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, port int) (edge, conc *proc) {

313		dir := t.TempDir()

315	psk = "%s"

318	name = "%s"
319	source_addr = "%s"

321	%s%s[wireguard]
322	private_key = "%s"

329	[log]
330	level = "error"
331	`, psk, auditPath.name, auditPath.edgeIP, amneziaProfileA, fecBlock, edgePriv, concPub, auditPath.concIP, port, concInner))

335

338	source_addr = "%s"

360		}
361		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
362		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
363		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
364		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
365		return edge, conc
366	}
367	
368	// ndpiFlow is one per-flow classification nDPI reported (from `ndpiReader -v 2`): the
369	// application protocol, its traffic category, and — decisively — the CONFIDENCE with
370	// which nDPI assigned it. A "Match by port" confidence is a PORT GUESS (it says nothing
371	// about the payload); a "DPI"/content confidence is a genuine payload/wire-format match.
372	type ndpiFlow struct {
373		proto      string
374		category   string
375		confidence string
376		raw        string // the trimmed source line, for diagnostics
377	}
378	
379	func (f ndpiFlow) String() string {
380		return fmt.Sprintf("proto=%q cat=%q confidence=%q", f.proto, f.category, f.confidence)
381	}
382	
383	// portGuess reports whether nDPI classified this flow purely by its destination port
384	// (Confidence: "Match by port") rather than by inspecting the payload. Such a guess is
385	// EXPECTED for UDP on WireGuard's registered port and must never fail the negative leg.
386	func (f ndpiFlow) portGuess() bool {
387		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(f.confidence), "match by port")
388	}
389	
390	// payloadVPN reports whether this flow is classified as a WireGuard/VPN protocol OR
391	// category by a PAYLOAD/CONTENT confidence — i.e. a genuine wire-format fingerprint, not
392	// a port guess. This is the single predicate both legs use: the positive control asserts
393	// it PRESENT (the detector has payload teeth); the negative leg asserts it ABSENT.
394	func (f ndpiFlow) payloadVPN() (label, pattern string, ok bool) {
395		if f.portGuess() {
396			return "", "", false
397		}
398		for _, c := range []string{f.proto, f.category} {
399			if pat, hit := matchVPNLabel(c); hit {
400				return c, pat, true
401			}
402		}
403		return "", "", false
404	}
405	
406	// payloadVPNFlow returns the first flow that is a payload-confidence WireGuard/VPN
407	// classification, if any.
408	func payloadVPNFlow(flows []ndpiFlow) (flow ndpiFlow, label, pattern string, ok bool) {
409		for _, f := range flows {
410			if label, pat, hit := f.payloadVPN(); hit {
411				return f, label, pat, true
412			}
413		}
414		return ndpiFlow{}, "", "", false
415	}
416	
417	func formatFlows(flows []ndpiFlow) string {
418		if len(flows) == 0 {
419			return "(none)"
420		}
421		parts := make([]string, len(flows))
422		for i, f := range flows {
423			parts[i] = f.String()
424		}
425		return strings.Join(parts, " | ")
426	}
427	
428	// nDPI per-flow field extractors. Each per-flow line (from `-v 2`) looks like:
429	//
430	//	1  UDP a:p <-> b:q [proto: 206/WireGuard][Stack: WireGuard]...[Confidence: DPI][FPC: 0/Unknown, Confidence: Unknown][cat: VPN/2]...
431	//
432	// ndpiFlowConfRE deliberately anchors on `[Confidence:` so it matches the PRIMARY
433	// confidence bracket and NOT the secondary `[FPC: …, Confidence: …]` first-packet-
434	// classification bracket (whose text does not start with "Confidence:").
435	var (
436		ndpiFlowProtoRE = regexp.MustCompile(`\[proto:\s*[^/\]]+/([^\]]+)\]`)
437		ndpiFlowCatRE   = regexp.MustCompile(`\[cat:\s*([^/\]]+)`)
438		ndpiFlowConfRE  = regexp.MustCompile(`\[Confidence:\s*([^\]]+)\]`)
439	)
440	
441	// runNdpiFlows runs `ndpiReader -v 2 -i <pcap>` (very verbose = per-flow detail) under a
442	// timeout and parses the per-flow classification lines. It fails if no flow is found (an
443	// empty capture or a changed output format would otherwise make the negative leg vacuous).
444	func runNdpiFlows(t *testing.T, pcap string) []ndpiFlow {
445		t.Helper()
446		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), dpiToolTimeout)
447		defer cancel()
448		out, err := exec.CommandContext(ctx, ndpiReaderBin, "-v", "2", "-i", pcap).CombinedOutput()
449		if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.DeadlineExceeded) {
450			t.Fatalf("ndpiReader timed out after %s on %s\n%s", dpiToolTimeout, pcap, out)
451		}
452		if err != nil {
453			t.Fatalf("ndpiReader on %s failed: %v\n%s", pcap, err, out)
454		}
455		flows := parseNdpiFlows(string(out))
456		if len(flows) == 0 {
457			t.Fatalf("ndpiReader produced no per-flow classification for %s — empty capture or output format changed:\n%s", pcap, out)
458		}
459		return flows
460	}
461	
462	// parseNdpiFlows extracts the per-flow classification lines from ndpiReader's very-verbose
463	// output. A per-flow line is identified by carrying both a `[proto: …]` and a
464	// `[Confidence: …]` field; the extractors pull the protocol name, category, and the
465	// primary confidence.
466	func parseNdpiFlows(out string) []ndpiFlow {
467		var flows []ndpiFlow
468		sc := bufio.NewScanner(strings.NewReader(out))
469		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 4*1024*1024) // per-flow lines can be long (histograms etc.)
470		for sc.Scan() {
471			line := sc.Text()
472			if !strings.Contains(line, "[proto:") || !strings.Contains(line, "[Confidence:") {
473				continue
474			}
475			f := ndpiFlow{raw: strings.TrimSpace(line)}
476			if m := ndpiFlowProtoRE.FindStringSubmatch(line); m != nil {
477				f.proto = strings.TrimSpace(m[1])
478			}
479			if m := ndpiFlowCatRE.FindStringSubmatch(line); m != nil {
480				f.category = strings.TrimSpace(m[1])
481			}
482			if m := ndpiFlowConfRE.FindStringSubmatch(line); m != nil {
483				f.confidence = strings.TrimSpace(m[1])
484			}
485			flows = append(flows, f)
486		}
487		return flows
488	}
489	
490	// suricataConfigArgs returns the `-c <config>` args to use, if a config is locatable:
491	// the WANBOND_SURICATA_CONFIG override first, then the standard install path. When
492	// neither exists it returns nil so suricata uses its own built-in config search.
493	func suricataConfigArgs() []string {
494		if c := os.Getenv(suricataConfigEnv); c != "" {
495			return []string{"-c", c}
496		}
497		if _, err := os.Stat(defaultSuricataConfig); err == nil {
498			return []string{"-c", defaultSuricataConfig}
499		}
500		return nil
501	}
502	
503	// runSuricata runs `suricata -r <pcap> -l <dir> -k none [-c <config>]` under a timeout and
504	// parses the resulting eve.json into the distinct app-layer protocols, alert
505	// signatures/categories, and the number of flow records it emitted. `-k none` disables
506	// checksum validation so offloaded-checksum captures (veth/loopback) are not dropped.
507	func runSuricata(t *testing.T, pcap string) (appProtos, signatures []string, flows int) {
508		t.Helper()
509		dir := t.TempDir()
510		args := []string{"-r", pcap, "-l", dir, "-k", "none"}
511		args = append(args, suricataConfigArgs()...)
512	
513		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), dpiToolTimeout)
514		defer cancel()
515		out, err := exec.CommandContext(ctx, suricataBin, args...).CombinedOutput()
516		if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.DeadlineExceeded) {
517			t.Fatalf("suricata timed out after %s on %s\n%s", dpiToolTimeout, pcap, out)
518		}
519		if err != nil {
520			t.Fatalf("suricata on %s failed: %v\n%s", pcap, err, out)
521		}
522	
523		eve := filepath.Join(dir, suricataEveFile)
524		data, rerr := os.ReadFile(eve)
525		if rerr != nil {
526			t.Fatalf("suricata wrote no %s at %s (a misconfigured suricata, not a pass): %v\n--- suricata output ---\n%s", suricataEveFile, eve, rerr, out)
527		}
528		return parseSuricataEve(t, data)
529	}
530	
531	// suricataEvent is the subset of an eve.json record this check inspects: the event type
532	// (to count decoded flows), the app-layer protocol suricata assigned the flow, and, for
533	// alert records, the rule that fired.
534	type suricataEvent struct {
535		EventType string `json:"event_type"`
536		AppProto  string `json:"app_proto"`
537		Alert     struct {
538			Signature string `json:"signature"`
539			Category  string `json:"category"`
540		} `json:"alert"`
541	}
542	
543	// parseSuricataEve parses newline-delimited eve.json into the distinct non-empty app-layer
544	// protocol values, alert signatures/categories, and the count of "flow" event records.
545	// Robust to the mixed event stream (flow/stats/alert): it keys off event fields.
546	func parseSuricataEve(t *testing.T, data []byte) (appProtos, signatures []string, flows int) {
547		t.Helper()
548		seenProto := map[string]struct{}{}
549		seenSig := map[string]struct{}{}
550		sc := bufio.NewScanner(strings.NewReader(string(data)))
551		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 4*1024*1024) // eve records can exceed the 64KB default
552		for sc.Scan() {
553			line := strings.TrimSpace(sc.Text())
554			if line == "" {
555				continue
556			}
557			var ev suricataEvent
558			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &ev); err != nil {
559				// A single malformed line is not a test failure by itself, but surfacing it
560				// aids diagnosis if the format shifted.
561				t.Logf("parseSuricataEve: skipping unparseable eve.json line: %v", err)
562				continue
563			}
564			if ev.EventType == "flow" {
565				flows++
566			}
567			if ev.AppProto != "" {
568				if _, ok := seenProto[ev.AppProto]; !ok {
569					seenProto[ev.AppProto] = struct{}{}
570					appProtos = append(appProtos, ev.AppProto)
571				}
572			}
573			for _, s := range []string{ev.Alert.Signature, ev.Alert.Category} {
574				if s == "" {
575					continue
576				}
577				if _, ok := seenSig[s]; !ok {
578					seenSig[s] = struct{}{}
579					signatures = append(signatures, s)
580				}
581			}
582		}
583		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
584			t.Fatalf("scan eve.json: %v", err)
585		}
586		return appProtos, signatures, flows
587	}
588	
589	// labelsHitVPN reports whether any string in ss matches a vpnLabelPattern.
590	func labelsHitVPN(ss []string) bool {
591		for _, s := range ss {
592			if _, ok := matchVPNLabel(s); ok {
593				return true
594			}
595		}
596		return false
597	}
598

content.content:
1	# wanbond.example.toml — exhaustive annotated configuration reference.
2	#
3	# Every key wanbond reads is present here. Copy this file, fill the <placeholders>,
4	# and delete what you do not need:
5	#
6	#   sudo install -m 0600 /dev/null /etc/wanbond/edge.toml
7	#   $EDITOR /etc/wanbond/edge.toml     # paste + edit this content
8	#
9	# 0600 is MANDATORY: the file holds the WireGuard private key and the PSK, so
10	# config.Load refuses any file whose mode is not exactly 0600.
11	#
12	# This example is EDGE-oriented. For a CONCENTRATOR: set role = "concentrator",
13	# uncomment [wireguard].listen_port, and REMOVE the peer `endpoint`/`endpoints`
14	# (the concentrator learns the edge's endpoints from authenticated traffic).
15	#
16	# Required keys are shown live; optional keys are commented-out with their
17	# DEFAULT value (uncommenting one at its shown value is a no-op). Cross-field
18	# constraints a single example cannot express inline are listed at the bottom.
19	# Full prose reference: docs/install.md §3z. Everything here is enforced at
20	# config load (fail-fast on the first violation) except the loopback + allowed_ips
21	# checks noted below.
22	
23	# ── top level ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24	role = "edge"                      # REQUIRED. "edge" | "concentrator". Never
25	                                   #   inferred from other keys.
26	psk  = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED. 32 raw bytes, base64. SAME value on
27	                                   #   both ends; keys the PSK-HMAC that
28	                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.
29	                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
30	# tun_persist = false              # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. When false the TUN
31	                                   #   wanbond0 is destroyed on daemon stop, so its
32	                                   #   addresses/routes/rules are dropped on every
33	                                   #   restart (today's teardown semantics). Set
34	                                   #   true and the daemon issues TUNSETPERSIST on
35	                                   #   start: wanbond0 SURVIVES a daemon restart
36	                                   #   with the SAME ifindex, so operator-owned
37	                                   #   addressing persists untouched. NOTE (D39): a
38	                                   #   persistent device STILL needs the
39	                                   #   NetworkManager unmanaged-devices drop-in on
40	                                   #   NM hosts — persistence keeps the link, it
41	                                   #   does not exempt it from NM.
42	
43	# ── paths: one [[paths]] block per WAN uplink; at least one is REQUIRED ───────
44	[[paths]]
45	name        = "starlink"           # REQUIRED. Stable, unique identifier.
46	source_addr = "192.168.1.10"       # REQUIRED. Bare local source IP the path's UDP
47	                                   #   socket binds to. Must be unique across paths
48	                                   #   (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE at the
49	                                   #   second bind). No default.
50	# dest_addr = "203.0.113.7:51820"  # OPTIONAL, edge-only meaning. Per-path
51	                                   #   concentrator endpoint (ip:port). Omit when
52	                                   #   one public IP fronts all uplinks (the peer's
53	                                   #   endpoint is reused). No default. Inert on the
54	                                   #   concentrator (it learns edge endpoints).
55	# link_bandwidth = "50Mbit"        # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared bottleneck
56	                                   #   bandwidth. SI bit/s: k/M/G = 1e3/1e6/1e9
57	                                   #   ("bit" may be written "bps"). Must be > 0.
58	                                   #   Used ONLY under weighted policy + pacing;
59	                                   #   inert otherwise. No default (undeclared).
60	# link_rtt = "45ms"                # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared baseline RTT (Go
61	                                   #   duration). REQUIRED (> 0) when link_bandwidth
62	                                   #   is set under weighted pacing; else ignored.
63	
64	# A second uplink (edge). Repeat the block per path.
65	[[paths]]
66	name        = "5g"
67	source_addr = "192.168.2.10"
68	
69	# ── wireguard: inner tunnel key material ─────────────────────────────────────
70	[wireguard]
71	private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"  # REQUIRED (both roles).
72	                                   #   Generate: wg genkey (public: … | wg pubkey)
73	# listen_port = 51820              # CONCENTRATOR-ONLY: REQUIRED (> 0) there; omit /
74	                                   #   leave 0 on the edge. uint16 (< 1024 needs
75	                                   #   CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE).
76	
77	# ── wireguard peers: at least one [[wireguard.peers]] is REQUIRED ─────────────
78	[[wireguard.peers]]
79	public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"  # REQUIRED. base64 32-byte key (the OTHER
80	                                   #   end's public key).
81	endpoint = "203.0.113.7:51820"     # EDGE: REQUIRED (this OR `endpoints`). ip:port
82	                                   #   literal only, no DNS. CONCENTRATOR: must be
83	                                   #   ABSENT (rejected) — it roams the edge
84	                                   #   dynamically. Mutually exclusive with
85	                                   #   `endpoints`.
86	# endpoints = ["203.0.113.7:51820", "198.51.100.7:51820"]
87	                                   # EDGE-ONLY ordered hub-failover list: [0] is the
88	                                   #   active concentrator, the rest ordered
89	                                   #   standbys. ip:port literals only, no DNS;
90	                                   #   duplicates rejected. Mutually exclusive with
91	                                   #   `endpoint` (its one-element form). Rejected on
92	                                   #   the concentrator.
93	allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]     # REQUIRED: >= 1 CIDR routed to this peer
94	                                   #   (enforced when the WG UAPI is built). This is
95	                                   #   the peer's inner tunnel address(es). A literal
96	                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 (full tunnel) is ALWAYS split
97	                                   #   into the equivalent /1+/1 pair at UAPI render —
98	                                   #   the engine never sees the literal /0.
99	# mode = "default-route"           # OPTIONAL, edge-only. Marks this peer as the
100	                                   #   edge's full-tunnel concentrator (opts into a
101	                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 / ::/0 allowed_ips entry above).
102	                                   #   Rejected on the concentrator. Config-surface
103	                                   #   only today: it does not itself install any
104	                                   #   OS-level default route.
105	
106	# ── amnezia obfuscation: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (plain WireGuard) ───────────
107	# ALL-OR-NOTHING: either omit the whole block, or set the entire jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2
108	# set — IDENTICALLY on both ends. One obfuscation engine per process.
109	# [amnezia]
110	# jc   = 4                         # junk packet count. > 0 when configured.
111	# jmin = 40                        # min junk packet size. > 0; jmin <= jmax.
112	# jmax = 70                        # max junk packet size. > 0.
113	# s1   = 30                        # init-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
114	# s2   = 40                        # response-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
115	                                   #   Constraint: (148 + s1) != (92 + s2) — the
116	                                   #   obfuscated init/response lengths must differ.
117	                                   #   (no defaults: required when the block present.)
118	# h1 = 1                           # magic header: initiation. Default 1..4 when the
119	# h2 = 2                           #   block is configured but NO header is given.
120	# h3 = 3                           #   When any is set, h1..h4 must be a DISTINCT set
121	# h4 = 4                           #   (values <= 4 mean "standard message type").
122	
123	# ── scheduler: OPTIONAL. Omitted => active-backup, all knobs below ignored ────
124	# [scheduler]
125	# policy = "active-backup"         # "active-backup" (DEFAULT) | "weighted". Every
126	                                   #   knob below applies ONLY to "weighted"; under
127	                                   #   active-backup they are inert and left unset.
128	# per_path_capacity_fps = 10000.0  # DEFAULT 10000. Reference per-path capacity
129	                                   #   (frame slots/s): aggregation-gate denominator
130	                                   #   and pacing refill rate. > 0.
131	# engage_fraction    = 0.9         # DEFAULT 0.9. Engage aggregation above
132	                                   #   engage_fraction * capacity. In (0, 1].
133	# disengage_fraction = 0.5         # DEFAULT 0.5. Collapse below disengage_fraction
134	                                   #   * capacity. Must be in [0, engage_fraction) —
135	                                   #   the hysteresis band.
136	# collapse_dwell = "2s"            # DEFAULT 2s. Sustained-low dwell before
137	                                   #   collapsing to primary-only. >= 0.
138	# load_tau = "200ms"               # DEFAULT 200ms. Offered-load rate estimator time
139	                                   #   constant. > 0.
140	# pacing_enabled = false           # DEFAULT false. Turn on per-path send-pacing
141	                                   #   (token buckets). Off => buckets bypassed.
142	# pacing_burst_frames = 64.0       # DEFAULT 64. Token-bucket burst (frames). > 0
143	                                   #   when pacing_enabled.
144	# weight_rtt_floor = "1ms"         # DEFAULT 1ms. RTT floor in the weight formula. >0.
145	# weight_loss_floor = 0.001        # DEFAULT 1e-3. Loss floor under the sqrt in the
146	                                   #   weight formula. > 0.
147	
148	# ── fec: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (no parity on the wire) ─────────────────────
149	# [fec]
150	# enabled = true                   # DEFAULT false. Turns the FEC plane on; when
151	                                   #   false every field below is ignored.
152	# data_shards = 10                 # K: inner datagrams per coding group. >= 1 when
153	                                   #   enabled. No default (required).
154	# parity_shards = 6                # M: parity frames per group (and the adaptive
155	                                   #   parity CEILING). >= 1 when enabled; K + M <=
156	                                   #   256. No default (required).
157	# deadline = "5ms"                 # DEFAULT 5ms. Partial-group flush deadline. > 0
158	                                   #   and <= 125ms.
159	# adaptive = false                 # DEFAULT false. Closed-loop parity controller
160	                                   #   (parity tracks measured loss up to the
161	                                   #   ceiling). Requires enabled = true.
162	# target_residual = 0.005          # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, primary sizing surface:
163	                                   #   post-recovery residual-loss SLA in (0, 1). No
164	                                   #   default. Mutually exclusive with safety_factor.
165	# safety_factor = 1.5              # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, legacy headroom multiplier >= 1.
166	                                   #   DEFAULTS to 1.5 when adaptive and NEITHER field
167	                                   #   is set. Must stay 0 in fixed mode. Mutually
168	                                   #   exclusive with target_residual.
169	
170	# ── dns: OPTIONAL. Omitted => system resolver, default cadence/timeouts ───────
171	# Selects the TRANSPORT a peer's opt-in hostname endpoint (dns = true, above)
172	# is resolved through. [dns] alone never turns hostname resolution on.
173	# [dns]
174	# resolver = "system"              # DEFAULT "system". "system" | "doh" | "dot".
175	# doh_url = "https://<ip>/dns-query"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "doh"; rejected
176	                                   #   under any other resolver. Must be https.
177	# dot_server = "<ip>"              # REQUIRED iff resolver = "dot"; rejected
178	                                   #   under any other resolver. Bare host, or
179	                                   #   "host:port" where port must be 853 (the
180	                                   #   IANA-assigned DoT port).
181	# bootstrap_ip = "<ip>"            # REQUIRED iff doh_url/dot_server's host is a
182	                                   #   hostname rather than an IP literal
183	                                   #   (BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: the private
184	                                   #   resolver's own address must be reachable
185	                                   #   WITHOUT a DNS lookup). REJECTED (must stay
186	                                   #   absent) when the host is already an IP
187	                                   #   literal. No default.
188	# poll_interval = "30s"            # DEFAULT 30s. Re-resolution cadence for an
189	                                   #   opted-in hostname peer endpoint. > 0.
190	# timeout = "5s"                   # DEFAULT 5s. Per-lookup bound. > 0.
191	
192	# ── metrics: OPTIONAL. Omit the block => no /metrics endpoint is served ───────
193	[metrics]
194	listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"          # No default. LOOPBACK-ONLY (127.0.0.0/8, ::1, or
195	                                   #   a hostname resolving only to loopback); any
196	                                   #   other address is REFUSED when the endpoint
197	                                   #   binds (not at load).
198	
199	# ── log: OPTIONAL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
200	[log]
201	level = "info"                     # DEFAULT "info" (empty => info). One of
202	                                   #   debug | info | warn | error ("warning"
203	                                   #   accepted for warn). Unknown => fail-fast.
204	
205	# ── Cross-field constraints (not expressible inline) ─────────────────────────
206	# • Roles: `role` is required, never inferred. Edge-only: per-peer endpoint/
207	#   endpoints (required on edge, rejected on concentrator) + the pacing
208	#   declarations (dest_addr is edge-only in effect, merely inert on the
209	#   concentrator). Concentrator-only: wireguard.listen_port (required there).
210	# • endpoint vs endpoints: mutually exclusive; `endpoint` is the one-element form
211	#   of `endpoints`. On the edge exactly one must be present; the concentrator sets
212	#   neither. Entries are ip:port literals only (no DNS), de-duplicated.
213	# • Distinct source_addr: each [[paths]] needs a unique, valid source_addr
214	#   (compared unmapped, so 192.0.2.10 and ::ffff:192.0.2.10 collide). `name` must
215	#   also be unique.
216	# • allowed_ips: >= 1 CIDR per peer; empty is rejected when the WG config is
217	#   assembled (not by config.validate).
218	# • amnezia all-or-nothing: an unconfigured (all-zero) block is plain WireGuard.
219	#   Once ANY of jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2/h1..h4 is set, the full jc,jmin,jmax,s1,s2 set
220	#   must be > 0, jmin <= jmax, 148+s1 != 92+s2, and h1..h4 must be a distinct set
221	#   (omitted headers default to 1..4). Same profile on both ends. One engine/process.
222	# • link_bandwidth + link_rtt are a pair: both-or-neither, and all-or-nothing
223	#   across every path (declare on every [[paths]] block or none). They take effect
224	#   ONLY under scheduler.policy = "weighted" with pacing_enabled = true; otherwise
225	#   a declared bandwidth is inert. When active they are mutually exclusive with the
226	#   raw per_path_capacity_fps / pacing_burst_frames knobs.
227	# • scheduler off-unless-present: no [scheduler] => active-backup, every weighted
228	#   knob ignored. Under weighted, the hysteresis band requires
229	#   disengage_fraction < engage_fraction.
230	# • fec off-unless-present: no [fec] (or enabled = false) => no parity. adaptive =
231	#   true requires enabled = true. In adaptive mode set EITHER target_residual OR
232	#   safety_factor, never both; both must stay 0 (unset) in fixed mode.
233	# • metrics loopback-only: listen must be a loopback address (or a hostname
234	#   resolving entirely to loopback); a non-loopback bind is refused when the
235	#   endpoint starts. Omit the block to serve no metrics.
236	# • dns off-unless-present: no [dns] => system resolver, default cadence/
237	#   timeouts; [dns] only selects the transport a peer's `dns = true` opt-in
238	#   uses, never a gate by itself. doh_url/dot_server are mode-specific: set
239	#   exactly the one matching `resolver`, the other (and bootstrap_ip under
240	#   resolver = "system") must stay absent. BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: a
241	#   hostname-form doh_url/dot_server host REQUIRES bootstrap_ip; an IP
242	#   literal host REJECTS a non-empty bootstrap_ip (mode mismatch).
243
